title: Wire CLI entrypoint and interactive TUI runtime
description: Add the `orchestrator tui` command and implement the thin interactive runtime loop that refreshes bead data on an interval, handles keyboard controls, keeps selection stable, scopes by optional feature root, and invokes the existing merge path with confirmation. Include degraded-mode handling when the rendering dependency is unavailable at runtime.
acceptance_criteria: `build_parser()` accepts `orchestrator tui` with `--feature-root` and `--refresh-seconds`, enforcing default 3 seconds and minimum 1 second.
The runtime opens a three-panel interface with bead list, selected bead details, and bottom status/activity information.

Refresh behavior rereads bead state from repository storage on the configured interval, shows newly ready beads and state transitions without restart, and preserves selection when the selected bead still exists.
Keyboard controls support j/k or arrow movement, filter cycling, manual refresh, merge confirmation flow, and quit.
Merge action delegates to existing merge behavior equivalent to `orchestrator merge <bead_id>`, rejects non-done beads with a clear status message, and keeps the UI running on merge failure.
If the TUI dependency is unavailable, the command prints a clear install hint, exits non-zero, and does not mutate bead state.

handoff_summary.completed: Wired a new `tui` CLI subcommand with `--feature-root` and validated `--refresh-seconds` parsing. Implemented a thin TUI runtime state layer and Textual app shell that renders bead list/detail/status panels, refreshes from repository storage on an interval, preserves selection by bead id, supports j/k and arrow navigation, filter cycling, manual refresh, merge confirmation, and quit. Integrated merge actions through the existing `command_merge` path and added non-mutating degraded-mode behavior when `textual` is unavailable.
handoff_summary.remaining: Interactive terminal behavior should be exercised in a real terminal session with `textual` installed; automated coverage here is unit-level and does not drive a live TUI session.
handoff_summary.risks: The sandbox did not run a live Textual UI, so layout and keybinding behavior are validated indirectly through unit tests and import-path checks rather than end-to-end terminal interaction.
handoff_summary.next_action: Have the review agent verify the CLI/runtime behavior and, if `textual` is available in the target environment, smoke-test `orchestrator tui` interactively.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Downstream work that also edits the CLI parser or `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` should re-run the TUI-focused unit tests because selection state, filter ordering, and merge confirmation logic are now coupled there.

conflict_risks: Downstream work that also edits the CLI parser or `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` should re-run the TUI-focused unit tests because selection state, filter ordering, and merge confirmation logic are now coupled there.

Added `orchestrator tui`, a Textual-backed interactive runtime with refresh/filter/navigation/merge controls, plus degraded-mode handling and regression coverage.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run targeted validation needed to confirm the implementation.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
